note: Static, connection-free CNA guidance. Federally accurate baseline; always confirm state specifics on your official Nurse Aide Registry when back online.

crisis.text: You are not alone. If you're exhausted, hopeless, or in crisis, reach out — support is available right now.

certificationBasics: Complete a state-approved nurse aide training program (federal minimum 75 hours, 16+ clinical; many states require more).
Pass the competency exam: a written/oral knowledge test plus a hands-on skills demonstration.
Clear a criminal background check and required health screenings (e.g., TB test, immunizations).
Get listed on your state's Nurse Aide Registry before working for pay.
Renew every 24 months with proof of paid nursing-related work.
scopeAndPrivacy: Stay within your scope of practice: report changes in condition to your supervising nurse; never give medical advice or medication.
Protect resident privacy (HIPAA): don't share names, room numbers, or record numbers outside care needs.
Document at the point of care — accurate, timely, objective.

prepStories: A time you de-escalated an agitated or anxious resident.
A fall or injury you helped prevent.
A teamwork or hand-off win during a busy shift.
A difficult family conversation you handled with empathy.
